title: Bridge Paper: Reductions in perceived stress following Transcendental Meditation practice are associated with increased brain regional connectivity at rest.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Language/Mantra** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Mantra — inner speech, focused attention meditation, chanting neural effects.
**Phase context:** mantra, mantra meditation, chanting, inner speech, focused attention meditation

Paper: Reductions in perceived stress following Transcendental Meditation practice are associated with increased brain regional connectivity at rest.
DOI: 10.1016/j.bandc.2020.105517
Abstract: Transcendental Meditation (TM) is defined as a mental process of transcending using a silent mantra. Previous work showed that relatively brief period of TM practice leads to decreases in stress and anxiety. However, whether these changes are subserved by specific morpho-functional brain modifications (as observed in other meditation techniques) is still unclear.
